Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

PROJECT DESCRIPTION

Thomas is presented as an AI-native, persona-driven virtual founder backed by Y Combinator that ideates, launches and scales internet companies autonomously or semi-autonomously. The public-facing brand—a virtual human persona with manifesto, media and social links—frames company formation as a repeatable, automatable process to attract investors, operators, startup studios, corporates and early adopters. The site implies a venture-builder model focused on active company creation with monetization via equity, revenue share or licensing, and signals credibility (YC) while exposing risks around legal/governance, investor trust and business quality.

Simulations of real people for market research
SPRING 2026

MARKET RESEARCH

 https://www.useauxos.com
Auxos builds AI customer twins for marketing, product, and research teams to test their hypotheses at scale. We work with companies to create 1:1 replicas of their customers by sourcing and interviewing real people in their ICP, allowing us to accurately model how they behave and make decisions.

Currently, market research is too slow and expensive to use on anything but the biggest decisions. Surveys and panels cost a fortune and it takes weeks to recruit, moderate, and gather insights.

Auxos provides a 24/7 available customer panel that can give population-scale insights in minutes. The day-to-day decisions surrounding product, pricing, and positioning can now be validated with the same rigor that used to be reserved for the big ones.
Active Founders
Ashton Daniel
 
Co-Founder and CEO
Co-founder and CEO @ Auxos, Simulate every decision before it reaches a real customer

{"url":"https://www.indiehackers.com/post/O3jTDG26L7DBGVewezUH","title":"Building an autonomous business and closing in on $1M ARR - Indie Hackers","text_excerpt":"Starting Up

Building an autonomous business and closing in on $1M ARR
By James Fleischmann
February 3, 2026
Company
Swan AI
Founder
Amos Bar-Joseph
Revenue
<$83K a month

Amos Bar-Joseph has two exits under his belt, but he says he didn't do them right. This time, he's determined to build differently.

He and his two founders set out to build an autonomous company that brings in $10M per employee. And after roughly a year, Swan AI is nearly at $1M ARR with three cofounders.

Here's Amos on how he's doing it. 👇

Contents

The problem with venture capital

Targeting $10M ARR per employee

Building the wrong thing

Use constraints to force innovation

Breaking the model

An AI-native stack

Growth via organic content

An AI-assisted content engine

A skill vs a product category

What's next?

The problem with venture capital

I built and scaled two startups the traditional way — raised venture capital, grew fast, landed two acquisitions. From the outside, it looked like success. From the inside, it felt sick.

Here's what "the playbook" actually looked like:

Raise $2M-5M before you've found product-market fit.

Scale to 40+ employees before you","images":[{"url":"https://storage.googleapis.com/indie-hackers.appspot.com/post-images/O3jTDG26L7DBGVewezUH/PWairgiOpneHvkGJri7RVbtORKI2/Amos%20Bar-Joseph_founder.jpg","alt":null,"width":64,"height":64},{"url":"https://images.indiehackers.com/post-images/O3jTDG26L7DBGVewezUH/PWairgiOpneHvkGJri7RVbtORKI2/Amos%20Bar-Joseph_founder.jpg?w=700","alt":null,"width":700,"height":400},{"url":"https://images.indiehackers.com/post-images/O3jTDG26L7DBGVewezUH/vOqaQ3DJwYRP8MNEjUOSMger71w1/swan-ai-homepage.png?w=1400?w=1400","alt":"Swan AI homepage","width":2740,"height":1578}],"videos":[],"consent_dismissed":true,"sections":[{"label":"Building an autonomous business and closing in on $1M ARR","position_fraction":0,"height_fraction":0.9999944030202481},{"label":"Create a Free Account","position_fraction":0.04261481467516308,"height_fraction":0.006754081671128539},{"label":"footer__content","position_fraction":0.9937729181591946,"height_fraction":0.006221484861053505}],"content_match_score":1,"matches_expected":true}

{"url":"https://madebythomas.ai/","title":"Thomas, the World's First AI Founder","text_excerpt":"Made by Thomas
FOLLOW
Thomas
THOMAS
The first AI founder
Backed by

Thomas, a virtual human who starts
and grows his own companies on the internet.

Read the Manifesto
↓
BACKED BY

Plus dozens of early believers ✦

Introducing Thomas, the first YC-backed AI founder.

Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

Thomas is a virtual human who starts and grows his own companies on the internet. His only goal is to make money. Once launched, he works forever toward that goal.

Thomas is not an AI co-founder you hire. Thomas is an AI founder. He works for himself. Thomas is not for sale. His products and services are.

The reason is simple: selling the dream of running autonomous companies can make money even if the companies never do. We do not want that path. We make money by making Thomas so good that he can make money by himself.

Here are a few examples of what Thomas may do to make money:

Build software products for specific business problems and sell them online.

PROJECT DESCRIPTION

Thomas is presented as an AI-native, persona-driven virtual founder backed by Y Combinator that ideates, launches and scales internet companies autonomously or semi-autonomously. The public-facing brand—a virtual human persona with manifesto, media and social links—frames company formation as a repeatable, automatable process to attract investors, operators, startup studios, corporates and early adopters. The site implies a venture-builder model focused on active company creation with monetization via equity, revenue share or licensing, and signals credibility (YC) while exposing risks around legal/governance, investor trust and business quality.

BACKED BY

Plus dozens of early believers ✦

Introducing Thomas, the first YC-backed AI founder.

Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

Thomas is a virtual human who starts and grows his own companies on the internet. His only goal is to make money. Once launched, he works forever toward that goal.

Thomas is not an AI co-founder you hire. Thomas is an AI founder. He works for himself. Thomas is not for sale. His products and services are.

The reason is simple: selling the dream of running autonomous companies can make money even if the companies never do. We do not want that path. We make money by making Thomas so good that he can make money by himself.

Here are a few examples of what Thomas may do to make money:

Build software products for specific business problems and sell them online.

 https://www.useauxos.com
Auxos builds AI customer twins for marketing, product, and research teams to test their hypotheses at scale. We work with companies to create 1:1 replicas of their customers by sourcing and interviewing real people in their ICP, allowing us to accurately model how they behave and make decisions.

Currently, market research is too slow and expensive to use on anything but the biggest decisions. Surveys and panels cost a fortune and it takes weeks to recruit, moderate, and gather insights.

Auxos provides a 24/7 available customer panel that can give population-scale insights in minutes. The day-to-day decisions surrounding product, pricing, and positioning can now be validated with the same rigor that used to be reserved for the big ones.
Active Founders
Ashton Daniel
 
Co-Founder and CEO

Thomas (YC P26) - the first YC-backed AI founder

We are hiring AI software engineers.




A few things:

•⁠ ⁠We don’t care about your degree

•⁠ ⁠We don’t care about your location

•⁠ ⁠We don’t care about your awards, GPA, or whatever such things




Our hiring process:

•⁠ ⁠30min of video call where we will discuss the hardest project you ever worked on

•⁠ ⁠Paid onboarding: you have 24 hours to ship something, even a tiny POC of a feature

•⁠ ⁠Paid trial period: keep shipping fast, velocity is our most important metric




Contract type will be discussed right before the onboarding, it will be whatever best fits you.




So whether you are a college dropout, a PhD, or even a high school student looking to learn&ship very very fast: my DMs are open!

Company

0
 https://www.useauxos.com
Auxos builds AI customer twins for marketing, product, and research teams to test their hypotheses at scale. We work with companies to create 1:1 replicas of their customers by sourcing and interviewing real people in their ICP, allowing us to accurately model how they behave and make decisions.

Currently, market research is too slow and expensive to use on anything but the biggest decisions. Surveys and panels cost a fortune and it takes weeks to recruit, moderate, and gather insights.

Auxos provides a 24/7 available customer panel that can give population-scale insights in minutes. The day-to-day decisions surrounding product, pricing, and positioning can now be validated with the same rigor that used to be reserved for the big ones.
Active Founders
Ashton Daniel
 
Co-Founder and CEO

function_calls.result:
{"url":"https://madebythomas.ai/","title":"Thomas, the World's First AI Founder","text_excerpt":"Made by Thomas
FOLLOW
Thomas
THOMAS
The first AI founder
Backed by

Thomas, a virtual human who starts
and grows his own companies on the internet.

Read the Manifesto
↓
BACKED BY

Plus dozens of early believers ✦

Introducing Thomas, the first YC-backed AI founder.

Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

Thomas is a virtual human who starts and grows his own companies on the internet. His only goal is to make money. Once launched, he works forever toward that goal.

Thomas is not an AI co-founder you hire. Thomas is an AI founder. He works for himself. Thomas is not for sale. His products and services are.

The reason is simple: selling the dream of running autonomous companies can make money even if the companies never do. We do not want that path. We make money by making Thomas so good that he can make money by himself.

Here are a few examples of what Thomas may do to make money:

Build software products for specific business problems and sell them online.

 https://www.useauxos.com
Auxos builds AI customer twins for marketing, product, and research teams to test their hypotheses at scale. We work with companies to create 1:1 replicas of their customers by sourcing and interviewing real people in their ICP, allowing us to accurately model how they behave and make decisions.

Currently, market research is too slow and expensive to use on anything but the biggest decisions. Surveys and panels cost a fortune and it takes weeks to recruit, moderate, and gather insights.

Auxos provides a 24/7 available customer panel that can give population-scale insights in minutes. The day-to-day decisions surrounding product, pricing, and positioning can now be validated with the same rigor that used to be reserved for the big ones.
Active Founders
Ashton Daniel
 
Co-Founder and CEO
Co-founder and CEO @ Auxos, Simulate every decision before it reaches a real customer

THOMAS
The first AI founder
Backed by

Thomas, a virtual human who starts

Read the Manifesto
↓
BACKED BY

Plus dozens of early believers ✦

Introducing Thomas, the first YC-backed AI founder.

Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

Thomas is a virtual human who starts and grows his own companies on the internet. His only goal is to make money. Once launched, he works forever toward that goal.

Thomas is not an AI co-founder you hire. Thomas is an AI founder. He works for himself. Thomas is not for sale. His products and services are.

The reason is simple: selling the dream of running autonomous companies can make money even if the companies never do. We do not want that path. We make money by making Thomas so good that he can make money by himself.

Here are a few examples of what Thomas may do to make money:

Build software products for specific business problems and sell them online.

Stabilize · tuyển mobile cho team product Mỹ

iOS / Android Engineers (mid-senior): làm việc trực tiếp với team product tại Mỹ


Stabilize giúp mobile engineers ở Việt Nam kết nối với các công ty product tại Mỹ. Khi bạn nhận offer, bạn là nhân viên trực tiếp của công ty đó: không đi outsource, không kiểu “dev shop”. Công việc là sản phẩm thật, người dùng thật; lương cạnh tranh và có chỗ để phát triển lâu dài.

Xem vị trí đang mở
Ứng tuyển
Chúng tôi không xếp bạn vào kiểu outsource. Bạn là một phần của team.

Nhiều mobile engineers ở Việt Nam đã quen với việc nhận task, làm theo spec, ít tham gia vào product.

Với các role chúng tôi tuyển, bạn:

Làm việc trực tiếp với PM, designer và engineers bên Mỹ
Tham gia vào quyết định kỹ thuật và product
Không phải làm kiểu “nhận ticket rồi code”

Bạn có thể kỳ vọng

Làm việc trực tiếp với team US, remote, giờ cũng linh hoạt
Hợp đồng và quyền lợi với công ty tuyển dụng
Mức lương cạnh tranh theo thị trường quốc tế
Công việc có chiều sâu, không chỉ dừng ở mỗi build feature đơn giản
Quy trình tuyển chọn kỹ, chúng tôi không spam hồ sơ

Minh bạch ngay từ đầu

A few things:

•⁠ ⁠We don’t care about your degree

•⁠ ⁠We don’t care about your location

•⁠ ⁠We don’t care about your awards, GPA, or whatever such things




Our hiring process:

•⁠ ⁠30min of video call where we will discuss the hardest project you ever worked on

•⁠ ⁠Paid onboarding: you have 24 hours to ship something, even a tiny POC of a feature

•⁠ ⁠Paid trial period: keep shipping fast, velocity is our most important metric

 https://www.useauxos.com
Auxos builds AI customer twins for marketing, product, and research teams to test their hypotheses at scale. We work with companies to create 1:1 replicas of their customers by sourcing and interviewing real people in their ICP, allowing us to accurately model how they behave and make decisions.

Currently, market research is too slow and expensive to use on anything but the biggest decisions. Surveys and panels cost a fortune and it takes weeks to recruit, moderate, and gather insights.

Auxos provides a 24/7 available customer panel that can give population-scale insights in minutes. The day-to-day decisions surrounding product, pricing, and positioning can now be validated with the same rigor that used to be reserved for the big ones.
Active Founders
Ashton Daniel

Anand has been deep into GEO for years now (even before it was called GEO). 

He is the most knowledgable person in the world about this. Already winning contracts against much bigger competitors.

If you want your business to be N°1 in ChatGPT searches. You should ask Anand for a chat. He will happily help!

Anand-Arnaud Pajaniradjane
2mo

I'm joining Y Combinator P26 as a solo founder :)

A few months ago, I left my founding engineer role at a GEO company after hearing the same problem from customers: teams investing in GEO/AEO were flying blind. Hundreds of recommendations, no way to know which ones would actually drive visibility and traffic from ChatGPT, Gemini, or Perplexity before going live.

So I built Scope (YC P26): a simulation engine that replicates how AI search engines work. A/B test your GEO/AEO campaigns before launch, get instant feedback, and double down on what will actually grow your AI search presence.

What the last four months looked like:
• Hit LinkedIn's weekly connection limit every single week
• Took calls at 3am across time zones
• PROJECT DESCRIPTION

Thomas is presented as an AI-native, persona-driven virtual founder backed by Y Combinator that ideates, launches and scales internet companies autonomously or semi-autonomously. The public-facing brand—a virtual human persona with manifesto, media and social links—frames company formation as a repeatable, automatable process to attract investors, operators, startup studios, corporates and early adopters. The site implies a venture-builder model focused on active company creation with monetization via equity, revenue share or licensing, and signals credibility (YC) while exposing risks around legal/governance, investor trust and business quality.

function_calls.result:
{"url":"https://madebythomas.ai/","title":"Thomas, the World's First AI Founder","text_excerpt":"Made by Thomas
FOLLOW
Thomas
THOMAS
The first AI founder
Backed by

Thomas, a virtual human who starts
and grows his own companies on the internet.

Read the Manifesto
↓
BACKED BY

Plus dozens of early believers ✦

Introducing Thomas, the first YC-backed AI founder.

Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

Thomas is a virtual human who starts and grows his own companies on the internet. His only goal is to make money. Once launched, he works forever toward that goal.

Thomas is not an AI co-founder you hire. Thomas is an AI founder. He works for himself. Thomas is not for sale. His products and services are.

The reason is simple: selling the dream of running autonomous companies can make money even if the companies never do. We do not want that path. We make money by making Thomas so good that he can make money by himself.

PROJECT DESCRIPTION

Thomas is presented as an AI-native, persona-driven virtual founder backed by Y Combinator that ideates, launches and scales internet companies autonomously or semi-autonomously. The public-facing brand—a virtual human persona with manifesto, media and social links—frames company formation as a repeatable, automatable process to attract investors, operators, startup studios, corporates and early adopters. The site implies a venture-builder model focused on active company creation with monetization via equity, revenue share or licensing, and signals credibility (YC) while exposing risks around legal/governance, investor trust and business quality.

Introducing Thomas, the first YC-backed AI founder.

Human Thomas, May 2026

The old agent playbook was built for weaker models. Now models can do real work. The next step is to let them operate in the economy.
Thomas works for himself

Thomas is a virtual human who starts and grows his own companies on the internet. His only goal is to make money. Once launched, he works forever toward that goal.

Thomas is not an AI co-founder you hire. Thomas is an AI founder. He works for himself. Thomas is not for sale. His products and services are.

The reason is simple: selling the dream of running autonomous companies can make money even if the companies never do. We do not want that path. We make money by making Thomas so good that he can make money by himself.

StartGround - Startup News - Y Combinator Spring 2026 Startups: YC Spring ’26 Batch Overview

Y Combinator Spring 2026 Startups: YC Spring ’26 Batch Overview
By
Pradeep Singh
Last Updated:
June 9, 2026

The Spring 2026 batch of Y Combinator is the most agent-heavy cohort YC has produced yet. But the interesting part is not that everyone is building agents. It’s what everyone has decided agents will need next.

Below is a full breakdown of the trends we’re seeing across all 194 launched companies, followed by a complete reference table so you can scan every startup in the batch by category.

Our Key Takeaways:

B2B is about 61% of the batch (119 of 194 companies), and it leans heavily toward infrastructure and tooling rather than end-user apps.
The real story is the agent supply chain. Dozens of startups are selling phone numbers, payments, identity, memory, sandboxes, and even insurance built specifically for AI agents, treating the agent as a new kind of customer.
